briefdescription.para: If TRUE, the 3 first time steps are elapsed.
Create the table of communication stats after the declaration of all of the counters.

detaileddescription.para: Counters stats are saved in the table communication_tracking_info Update the entire table communication_tracking_info : the total time of communication for all communication counter and the communication time per domain j for each counter /!\ reset the counter associated with cid comm_domaines_on becomes true

briefdescription.para: Start the count of a counter.

parameterdescription.para: The ID of the counter that the user want to start. It is a global variable that is declare in stats_counters.cpp
Indicate if the user want to record the communication associated with the counter, set to true by default

detaileddescription.para: Compute the average, minimum, maximum and variance of the elapsed time on an iteration for each counter

parameterdescription.para: Number of time step of the calculation

briefdescription.para: Aggregate stats of the counter associated with counter ID to the stats in a Stat_Results type object.

parameterdescription.para: Global variable link to a specific counter. It is declare in stats_counters.cpp
A Stat_Results type object used to store the stats concerning the wanted counter

briefdescription.para: Delete the table of communication stats at the end of the calculation.
Function that create the file CASE_NAME_log_perf.csv.

detaileddescription.para: This function construct a .csv file in which counter stats are printed.

This function prints the global and detailed performance log, depending on the debug_level. It has to be called on every processor simultaneously During this function, all counters are stop and then restart
message_info
String that state the overall step of calculation we are in : initialization, resolution or post-processing

inbodydescription.para: < Stringstream that contains stats for each processor
< Stringstream that contains stats average on the processors : processor number = -1
< Stringstream that contains the lines at the start of the file
< number of item of a line of the _csv.Tu file
< Contains the the width of the printed string, 20 for numbers by default
< Contains the data of a line that we want to print in the _csv.TU file.
< Create a temporary stringstream for converting wanted line items in string to construct the line_items vector and therefore
We specify the width of large items of lines of the _csv.Tu file for making it readable by human
Then we create a vector line_items that contains each item we want to print
After filling line_items and item_size, we use the function build_line_csv to build the line at the expected format
Check if all of the processors see the same number of counter, if not print an error message in perfs_globales
< If min_nb_of_counters != max_nb_of_counters, aggregated stats are not printed
Time of reference for statistics computation
Compute Divers (time not counted by other counters in time step loop):
Look for counter Divers
Compute CPU Divers = counter(time step) - sum(other counters))
< Level of details of the counter
< Equal to 1 if the counter is a communication counter, 0 otherwise
< number of time the counter is open and closed
< A custom quantity which depends on the counter. Used for example to compute the bandwidth
< Percent of the total time used in the method tracked by the counter
< the standard dev of all the prev vars
< Convert into string the item we want to print in the line, here the overall simulation step
< Add the item to the vector line_itmes, used to construct the line of the _csv.TU file
< Empties the temporary stringstream
< Add the processor number to the vector line_items
< Add the counter's family to the vector line_items, null if the counter does not belong in a family
< Add the counter's name to the vector line_items
< Add the counter's level to the vector line_items
< Add 1 if the counter is a communication counter, 0 otherwise
< Add the percent of total time used by the operation tracked by counter i to the vector line_items
< Time elapsed when using the operation tracked by counter i
< Detail per proc, so the min, max, avg and SD on proc is equal to 0
< Detail per proc, so the min, max, avg and SD on proc is equal to 0
< Detail per proc, so the min, max, avg and SD on proc is equal to 0
< Number of time the counter was called on the overall simulation step
< Detail per proc, so the min, max and SD on proc is equal to 0
< Detail per proc, so the min, max and SD on proc is equal to 0
< Detail per proc, so the min, max and SD on proc is equal to 0
< Averaged time elapsed by time step for the operation tracked by the counter on the overall simulation step
< Minimum time elapsed by time step for the operation tracked by the counter on the overall simulation step
< Maximum time elapsed by time step for the operation tracked by the counter on the overall simulation step
< Standard Deviation of time elapsed by time step for the operation tracked by the counter on the overall simulation step
< Custom variable that depends on the counter the overall simulation step
< Detail per proc, so the min, max and SD on proc is equal to 0
< Detail per proc, so the min, max and SD on proc is equal to 0
< Detail per proc, so the min, max and SD on proc is equal to 0
Extract information of each counter
Update the time statistics of communication counters using the table communication_tracking_info as if GET_COMM_DETAILS is equal to 1, the stats in stat_internals are not up to date
< Finds the index associated with the counter i in the communication_tracking_info table
< Counter with id == 0 corresponds to the total time
< Update total_time_
< The first three time steps are disregard , id==2 corresponds to the counter associated with the loop that compute the time step
< Build the line of the stats associated on the counter i for a single proc
Min of each quantity on the set of processor
Max of each quantity on the set of processor
Average of each quantity on the set of processor
Standard Deviation of each quantity on the set of processor
< Build the line of the aggregated stats on the processor of the counter i
< Equal to 1 if the counter is a communication counter, 0 otherwise
Aggregated stats by family
< Build the line of the stats associated on the counter i for a single proc
Min of each quantity on the set of processor
Max of each quantity on the set of processor
Average of each quantity on the set of processor
Standard Deviation of each quantity on the set of processor
< Build the line of the aggregated stats on the processor of the counter i

briefdescription.para: Update communications info regarding counter cid and stop the count.

detaileddescription.para: Update the variable communication_tracking_info[i][cid+1] with the communication tracking associated with counter cid. comm_domaines_on become flase

memberdef.argsstring: (const Stat_Counter_Id &counter_id, trustIdType quantity=0, int count=1, bool track_comm=true)
memberdef.name: end_count

briefdescription.para: End the count of a counter and update values in the Stat_Internals object of the Statistiques object.

detaileddescription.para:
counter_id
The ID of the counter that the user want to start. It is a global variable that is declare in stats_counters.cpp
quantity
A user defined quantity which depends on the counter (can be a number of iteration, a buffer size,.... It is set to zero by default @count Parameter that count the number of time the counter has been used between begin_count and end_count. By default equal to 1.
track_comm
Indicate if the user want to record the communication associated with the counter, set to true by default
On ajoute quantity a la somme des 'quantity' (par defaut 0) stockees pour ce compteur et on ajoute 'count' (par defaut 1) au compteur d'appels a end_count pour ce compteur (quantity sert a sommer par exemple la taille des donnees traitees pendant ce comptage et count sert a compter combien de fois on a appele ce compteur)

briefdescription.para: Get back the stats of a counter and store them in a Stat_Resluts type object.

parameterdescription.para: Global variable link to a specific counter. It is declare in stats_counters.cpp
A Stat_Results type object used to store the stats concerning the wanted counter

briefdescription.para: Get back and agregate the stats of a set of counters and store them in a Stat_Resluts type object.

parameterdescription.para: Name of a set of counter for which we want aggregated stats. It is declare in stats_counters.cpp
A Stat_Results type object used to store the stats concerning the wanted counter

briefdescription.para: Get the protected variable total_time of a Statistics type object.
Give the last interval of time the counter has been used.

detaileddescription.para:
If the counter is still running, return the time since the last begin_count If the counter is stopped, return the interval of time of the last count
counter_id
The ID of the counter that the user want to start. It is a global variable that is declare in stats_counters.cpp

memberdef.argsstring: (int level, const char *const description, const char *const familly=0, int comm=0)
memberdef.name: new_counter

briefdescription.para: Create a new counter in the Statistiques object.

detaileddescription.para:
This function has to be called simultaneously on every processor
level
If level <= debug_level, then the statistics of this counter will be printed at the end.
decsription
String used as the counter name/short description
familly
Parameter to regroup counter together (if they have the same family number) in order to create aggregated stats ; if equal to zero, then no aggregation
comm
If equal to 1, then the counter is a communication counter, otherwise, it is a "basic" counter. Set to 0 by default

For each declared communication domain/function, print the average time spend for each type of communication on each processor And for each time of communication, print the average time spend on each domain/function on each processor

briefdescription.para: Reset the counter related to the counter_id.

detaileddescription.para: Some variables are kept even after the reset : counters_avg_min_max_var_per_step and communication_tracking_info
Some variables are kept even after the reset : counters_avg_min_max_var_per_step and communication_tracking_info

briefdescription.para: Function that change the debug level.

detaileddescription.para:
This function have to be called simultanely on each processor
level
the new debug level During the printing of the performance, the debug level is set to 0
level
the new debug level During the printing of the performance, the debug level is set to 0
Cette methode renvoie un temps en secondes depuis une origine qui depend de la methode de mesure de la classe Time.

memberdef.argsstring: (const int id_, trustIdType quantity, int count)
memberdef.name: end_count_
